content.claim: Teacher unions are internally divided on the future of 6/8-year gimnáziums: the president of the Pedagógusok Szakszervezete (PSZ) expressed support for phasing the system out, while a PDSZ representative emphasized addressing segregation more broadly, preventing unions from acting as a unified veto player or enabling coalition on this specific issue.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Only two union representatives' positions are documented; the union memberships themselves have not been surveyed
It is unclear whether these divergent views reflect genuine policy disagreement or tactical positioning within a live political dispute
No evidence provided on the political weight or organizing capacity each union can deploy on this issue
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
